content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN **distinct** integers: [A1,A2,…,AN][A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N][A1​,A2​,…,AN​].

We define f(X)f(X)f(X) as the index iii which has the minimum value of ∣Ai−X∣|A_i - X|∣Ai​−X∣ among i=1,2,…,Ni = 1, 2, \ldots, Ni=1,2,…,N. In case there are multiple indices iii with the minimum value, choose the one with the smallest value of AiA_iAi​.

Your task is to count the number of integers XXX such that f(X)=if(X) = if(X)=i for each i=1,2,…,Ni = 1, 2, \ldots, Ni=1,2,…,N.

In case the answer is infinite for some index iii, output −1-1−1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer - NNN, the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN **distinct** integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN integers, the number of integers XXX such that f(X)=if(X) = if(X)=i for each i=1,2,…,Ni = 1, 2, \ldots, Ni=1,2,…,N, or −1-1−1 if the answer is infinite.
constraints: Ai≠AjA_i \ne A_jAi​=Aj​ for all 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i \lt j \le N1≤i<j≤N
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It can be shown that for X>5X > 5X>5, f(X)=1f(X) = 1f(X)=1, and thus there are infinite integers for i=1i = 1i=1. Similarly, for all X<2X < 2X<2, f(X)=2f(X) = 2f(X)=2.
Test case 222: Only for the integers X=5,6,7,8X = 5, 6, 7, 8X=5,6,7,8, f(X)=1f(X) = 1f(X)=1, and thus the output is 444 for i=1i = 1i=1.
